claim: Hand A's vocabulary splits into two statistically distinct populations consistent with a 15th-century nomenclator cipher: a HIGH-FREQUENCY class (function words, connectors) that follows natural-language statistics, and a LOW-FREQUENCY class (technical terms / codebook entries) that does NOT — showing flatter glyph distribution, tighter word-length variance, and lower phonotactic structure than the high-frequency class. If confirmed, the nomenclator hypothesis explains why every tested character-substitution mapping has failed (the codebook entries cannot be decoded by any letter-level substitution), why coverage-based glossaries reach 80%+ (the function-word half is decodable), and why Hand A passes internal natural-language tests (the grammatical scaffold IS natural language).
evidence_for: 12 language mappings refuted on Hand A under shuffle-test (`H-BV-HAND-A-LANGUAGE-SCREEN-02`) — character-level substitution does not work

Pagel's 81-term map clears the +0.010 shuffle threshold (`H-BV-HAND-A-MAP-COMPARISON-01`) but expansion to 167 terms collapses to +0.0030 (`H-BV-PAGEL-EXPANSION-01`) — consistent with the small head being decodable function-words, the long tail being arbitrary codes
Hand A passes natural-language structural tests (`H-BV-HAND-A-INTERNAL-01`: inflection 2.46, LDA purity 0.831, bigram specificity 1.44x) — suggesting the grammatical scaffold is real natural language

Per-plant content decoding refuted across five tests (`H-BV-SYLLABLE-LEVEL-01`, `H-BV-HAND-A-CONTENT-INDEPENDENCE-01`, `H-BV-CONSONANT-ID-01`, `H-BV-CONSONANT-SEMANTICS-01`, `H-BV-PERUN-NULL-01`) — content words may be opaque codes
15th-century Italian diplomatic practice (Florence, Venice, Milan, Padua) made the nomenclator the dominant cipher form; the manuscript's provenance is Northern Italian
evidence_against: A two-population vocabulary split is also produced by ordinary natural language (function vs content); the test must show the low-frequency class is MORE flat/uniform than natural-language content words, not just different from function words

If the codebook tail is arbitrary, hapax ratio should be very high in the tail — but Hand A's hapax ratio (0.701) is already corpus-wide, so this is consistent with both natural language and nomenclator
The Voynich precedes the standard Cicco-Simonetta nomenclator codification by ~20 years; it would be an early or transitional form if a nomenclator at all
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE on Hand A only:

  (1) BUILD type-frequency table: count every token in Hand A. Sort types by descending frequency.
  (2) HEAD/TAIL SPLIT at the rank R where cumulative token count first reaches 50% of total Hand-A tokens. HIGH = ranks 1..R (head), LOW = ranks R+1..end (tail). The HIGH class is small (~50-200 types) but covers ~50% of tokens; the LOW class is large (~3000+ types) and covers the other 50%.
  (3) MEASURES per class:
        (a) Zipf exponent: linear regression of log(rank within class) vs log(frequency); report slope.
        (b) Glyph entropy: Shannon entropy of the glyph-unit distribution computed over the EVA-tokenised glyphs of all WORDS in that class (weighted by token count).
        (c) Mean word length and standard deviation in glyph-units.
        (d) Type-glyph entropy: entropy of glyph-units treating each TYPE as an iid sample (unweighted) — gauges code-set uniformity.
  (4) GAPS: compute (HIGH - LOW) for each measure. Nomenclator predicts:
        - Zipf gap > 0 (high-freq class steeper)
        - Glyph entropy gap < 0 (low-freq class FLATTER, hence higher entropy)
        - Mean length gap < 0 (low-freq class LONGER on average)
        - Length stddev gap > 0 (low-freq class more UNIFORM length, lower stddev)
        - Type-glyph entropy gap < 0 (low-freq class more uniform)
  (5) PERMUTATION NULL: randomly relabel each TYPE as HIGH or LOW preserving class sizes; recompute all gaps; repeat 1000 times. Two-tailed p-value for each gap = fraction of null gaps at least as extreme as observed in the nomenclator-predicted direction.
test_threshold: CONFIRMED if 4 of 5 nomenclator predictions hold AND at least 2 of those have permutation p < 0.01 in the predicted direction. MARGINAL if 2-3 of 5 hold with at least one p < 0.05. REFUTED if 0-1 of 5 predictions hold or all gaps fall within the natural-language permutation null at p > 0.05.
tests_remaining: Execute locked nomenclator vocabulary-split test in separate commit after lock
reasoning: Twelve language mappings refuted, abjad chi-square under-discriminating, Brady's Hand-A-only result marginal, Pagel's only-the-head clears threshold while expansion fails. The pattern is consistent with a hybrid cipher in which the high-frequency vocabulary is real natural language (under some substitution that may be partially decodable) and the low-frequency vocabulary is arbitrary code. Nomenclator was the dominant 15th-century Italian diplomatic cipher form; testing it explicitly is overdue. The vocabulary-split test runs in one cycle on existing corpus data and either redirects effort toward dual-corpus reconstruction (nomenclator) or eliminates it.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-16 user-directed pre-registration following 8-hypothesis domain-informed list (#7)
